MAPS Celebrates 40 Years at a Defining Moment for Psychedelics
outcome_summary: MAPS marks its 40th anniversary, having raised more than $150 million, supported 45 studies, trained 500+ therapists, and contributed to the first NDA submission for a psychedelic-assisted therapy. The organization also notes a new federal Executive Order directing agencies to expand psychedelic research and accelerate regulatory approval.

title: MAPS Welcomes Federal Action to Advance Science-Driven Psychedelic Research and Policy
outcome_summary: On April 18, 2026, MAPS issued a statement welcoming an Executive Order directing $50 million in federal funds to accelerate research into psychedelic compounds including ibogaine, psilocybin, LSD, and MDMA for serious mental illness, while emphasizing the need for rigorous science, ethical frameworks, and Indigenous community collaboration.

title: Not Many Patents Threaten Traditional Knowledge About Ayahuasca
outcome_summary: A detailed survey indicates that active patent requests implying undue appropriation of ayahuasca-based traditional knowledge are rare; the article argues that inaccurate or exaggerated information about patent threats spreads alarmism without factual basis and hinders negotiation between Indigenous communities and researchers.

title: An Uneasy Partnership?
outcome_summary: Ibogaine is increasingly paired with 5-MeO-DMT in retreat settings, which complicates research data; ibogaine has shown improvements in executive function and reductions in PTSD, depression, and anxiety symptoms in study participants. Funding for ibogaine research has increased, including $50 million allocated in Texas.

title: Psychedelic Renaissance Progresses to Psychedelic Enlightenment
Football Player Jordan Poyer Leads the Breath of Life Tour
outcome_summary: NFL player Jordan Poyer founded Luminate in 2024 and launched the "Breath of Life" tour, a modern ceremonial concert blending breathwork, sound healing, movement, and live music, with MAPS as a founding sponsor. The tour aims to scale access to psychedelic-informed healing modalities.

title: Trump Signs Executive Order To Fast-Track Psychedelic Research
institution: White House / U.S. Department of Health and Human Services / Department of Veterans Affairs
outcome_summary: President Trump signed an executive order on April 18, 2026, directing federal agencies to reduce regulatory barriers and streamline FDA approval for psychedelic therapies, with a specific $50 million federal initiative to study ibogaine for veteran mental health and substance use disorders.

title: Racing Beyond Racemic: A Look at Ketamine Drug Development Ahead of atai's PCN-101 Phase 2a Readout
What Approval Won’t Solve: Brian Barnett on Ketamine’s Lessons, RVUs, and Scaling Psychedelic Care
Therapy or Threat? UK Expert Warns of Rising Ketamine Addiction as Phase 3 Alcoholism Trial Launches
outcome_summary: Phase III trial for ketamine-assisted therapy in alcohol addiction has launched. Professor Celia Morgan of University of Exeter, who has led the world's largest studies of KAT, discusses the therapeutic potential alongside rising concerns about recreational ketamine addiction in the UK.

title: Protocol for intravenous ketamine treatment for patients with therapy-resistant depression
institution: Østfold Hospital (The Ketamine Unit at DPS Nordre, Østfold Hospital)
outcome_summary: Protocol describing intravenous ketamine treatment program for therapy-resistant depression at Østfold Hospital, including indication criteria (MADRS ≥20, failed ≥2 antidepressants), treatment procedures, dosage, psychotherapeutic integration, and safety monitoring framework. Version 1 dated November 2024.

title: UCSF Psilocybin Clinical Trials — San Francisco Bay Area
MDMA Trials for PTSD in Soldiers, What to Know Now
outcome_summary: The FDA has reviewed Phase 3 trial data; as of 2026, MDMA has not been approved for general clinical use. The VA does not currently offer MDMA-assisted therapy as a standard treatment, though clinical trials continue through approved research sites.

title: RFK, Jr., puts psychedelics on fast track to FDA review and approval
outcome_summary: The FDA announced it is fast-tracking review and approval for three companies studying psilocybin and methylone for depression and PTSD, and approved a phase 1 clinical trial of noribogaine for alcohol use disorder.

title: Why Trump reversed course to fast-track psychedelic drugs for mental healthcare
outcome_summary: In April 2026, President Donald Trump signed an executive order to accelerate research into psychedelic drugs for mental illness and issued priority review vouchers to three companies developing psychedelic or MDMA-like therapies (Compass Pathways, Usona Institute, and Transcend Therapeutics) to speed FDA review processes.

title: VA clinical trial on psychedelic-assisted therapy gives Missouri lawmakers hope
outcome_summary: The VA launched a new randomized controlled trial of MDMA-assisted therapy for PTSD and alcohol use disorder in U.S. veterans; it will enroll approximately 80 veterans and compare outcomes between those receiving MDMA-assisted therapy and a control group.

title: VA launches MDMA-assisted mental health therapy trial
outcome_summary: The trial was announced to evaluate safety and efficacy of MDMA-assisted therapy for PTSD and alcohol use disorder in U.S. Veterans. It will enroll approximately 80 Veterans and compare outcomes between MDMA-assisted therapy and identical psychotherapy with an active placebo.

title: Brain scans reveal how ketamine quickly lifts severe depression
institution: Yokohama City University Graduate School of Medicine
outcome_summary: PET imaging with [11C]K-2 tracer showed that ketamine's antidepressant effects in treatment-resistant depression are mediated by dynamic, region-specific changes in AMPA receptor density in the living human brain, with some cortical areas showing increased receptor density and reward-processing regions like the habenula showing reductions.

title: Drug combo is the first to prolong ketamine’s antisuicide effects
outcome_summary: A drug combination was found to be the first to prolong ketamine's antisuicide effects, though further research is needed to understand longer term effects and applicability to other types of depression such as bipolar disorder.

title: Is Ketamine Still a Party Drug in 2026?
outcome_summary: Article discusses ketamine's dual identity in 2026 as both a continuing recreational/party drug and an established medical treatment for mental health conditions. No specific study results or clinical trial outcomes reported.

title: Studies currently recruiting | Johns Hopkins Medicine
Serial Ketamine Infusions as Adjunctive Therapy to Inpatient Care ...
MAPS Welcomes Federal Action to Advance Science-Driven Psychedelic Research and Policy
outcome_summary: MAPS issued a statement welcoming a federal Executive Order directing $50 million in funding to accelerate research on psychedelic compounds including ibogaine, psilocybin, LSD, and MDMA for serious mental illness, while emphasizing the need for rigorous science, ethical frameworks, and collaboration with Indigenous communities.

title: Psychedelics & Healing Initiative Trends for 2026
outcome_summary: The article provides an industry overview of psychedelic medicine trends, noting that eight psychedelic candidates have received FDA 'breakthrough therapy' designations and seven have reached FDA Phase 3 trials. It highlights increasing bipartisan political support and FDA receptiveness to novel psychedelic compounds for integration into the healthcare system.

title: Psilocybin Patent Tracker
BREAKING: Second Positive Phase 3 for Compass' Psilocybin, Though Modest Magnitude Raises Questions
outcome_summary: COMPASS Pathways reported a second positive Phase 3 trial for its synthetic psilocybin (COMP360) in treatment-resistant depression, though the modest magnitude of effect and adverse event profile raised questions among analysts and investors.

title: Australia’s Largest Private Health Insurer Commits $10M to Fund MDMA Therapy for PTSD in First-of-its-Kind Program
outcome_summary: Medibank committed AUD $10 million to fund MDMA-assisted therapy for PTSD and a long-term outcomes and health economics study led by Australian National University researchers, partnering with Emyria to pilot the program.

title: Confirmatory Phase 3 Trial of MDMA Therapy for PTSD is “Successful”, Says MAPS
outcome_summary: Results confirmed prior positive findings from MAPP1; no serious adverse events were observed. More than half of participants identified as people of color.

title: Vibe Check: FDA Set to Decide Future of MDMA-Assisted Therapy This Week
outcome_summary: Pending FDA decision on New Drug Application (NDA) for MDMA-assisted therapy for PTSD with target PDUFA date of August 11, 2024; three possible outcomes discussed: approval, Complete Response Letter, or delay.

title: Evidence and Experience: Samuel Wilkinson on the State of (Es)ketamine Therapy and What Comes Next
outcome_summary: Samuel Wilkinson of Yale is leading a large multi-site, open-label, randomized non-inferiority study comparing IV ketamine to intranasal esketamine (Spravato) for treatment-resistant depression. No comparative outcomes are yet reported; existing observational data suggesting IV ketamine may perform better are limited by non-randomized design and socioeconomic confounding.

title: Compass Pathways Announces Second Quarter 2025 Financial Results and Business Highlights
outcome_summary: Compass Pathways reported positive primary endpoint results from its first COMP360 Phase 3 trial (COMP005) in treatment-resistant depression, showing a highly statistically significant and clinically meaningful reduction in depression at 6 weeks with no unexpected safety findings. The second Phase 3 trial (COMP006) continues to enroll, with 26-week data expected in late 2026.

title: Breaking Trauma: Mechanisms of MDMA-Assisted Psychotherapy for PTSD
UCSF Ketamine Clinical Trials — San Francisco Bay Area
outcome_summary: This is a landing page listing multiple UCSF clinical trials involving ketamine across various conditions including Parkinson's disease, depression, status epilepticus, eating disorders, and methamphetamine use disorder.

title: Just a moment...
Extended-release ketamine tablets for treatment-resistant depression: a randomized placebo-controlled phase 2 trial
outcome_summary: The primary endpoint was met; the 180 mg extended-release ketamine tablet group showed a least square mean difference in MADRS score of -6.1 (95% CI 1.0 to 11.16, P = 0.019) compared to placebo at 13 weeks. Relapse rates showed a dose response from 70.6% for placebo to 42.9% for 180 mg. The treatment was well tolerated with no blood pressure changes, minimal sedation and dissociation.

title: Johns Hopkins Center for Psychedelic and Consciousness Research
institution: Johns Hopkins Center for Psychedelic and Consciousness Research
outcome_summary: The center focuses on exploring psilocybin for various psychiatric and behavioral disorders, with demonstrated safety in regulated settings and potential benefits for anxiety in cancer patients and smoking cessation.
